howItWorks.intro: This insurance deductible calculator uses standard formulas to provide accurate results.
rules: Total Cost = Annual Premiums + min(Claims, Deductible)
howItWorks.outro: Insurance rates depend on many individual factors. Get personalized quotes from licensed providers.

faq.question: Should I choose a high or low deductible?
faq.answer: High deductible = lower premiums but more out of pocket when you have a claim. Choose high if you are healthy and have savings to cover the deductible. Choose low if you have frequent medical needs.

faq.question: What is a deductible?
faq.answer: The amount you pay out of pocket before insurance kicks in. A $1,000 deductible means you pay the first $1,000 of covered expenses each year.

faq.question: How do I calculate the break-even point?
faq.answer: Compare total annual cost (premiums + deductible) for each plan. The plan with the lower total cost wins. If claims are low, high deductible usually wins.

faq.question: What about HSA eligibility?
faq.answer: High-deductible health plans (HDHPs) qualify for Health Savings Accounts. HSA contributions are tax-deductible, grow tax-free, and withdrawals for medical expenses are tax-free — a triple tax benefit.
